summary_one_line: A ClassDef table whose 16-bit format selector equals 4, reachable via a valid GDEF table, is required to take the case-4 branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The ClassDef table's 16-bit big-endian 'format' field (the switch operand u.format) must equal the exact value 4, reached through a well-formed GDEF table containing a glyph-class ClassDef of format 4.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winning cmplog seed carries the literal 'GDEF' table tag and structured table bytes including a 00 04 word; it resolves the branch in all 10/10 trials with avg hitcount 162 and only this case-4 arm taken (L2000 T=54 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds are tiny (6-63 bytes) and random/zero-filled with no GDEF tag or format==4 selector; they never reach case 4 and instead fall through to the default arm (L2002 T=1620 F=0), blocked in all 10/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the switch comparison against 4 via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp, logging the constant operand; I2SRandReplace then substitutes the literal 4 into the format-field offset of the input, which blind havoc byte mutation in naive cannot reliably hit at the exact 16-bit offset.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the case-4 arm of a switch on the ClassDef format field.
The blocker source line is the case 4 dispatch into format4.get_class.
The winning seed contains the literal GDEF table tag plus a 00 04 word, satisfying the format-selector requirement.
The winner resolves the branch in all trials with high hitcount while the loser is blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=162  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The winner takes only the case-4 true arm while the loser only takes the default arm, never case 4.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2000  T=54 F=0  T=0 F=1620  case 4: return u.format4.get_class (glyph_id);  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser naive seeds are tiny and unstructured, lacking any GDEF table tag or format-4 selector.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=003817559785f774, size=6 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=0s, mutation_op=ByteDecMutator,WordAddMutator):

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog intercepts integer comparisons and feeds operands to I2SRandReplace, which substitutes one CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S capability, only blind havoc and token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5483 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 3 --seed 2b85cc197690055a` and observed exactly 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 26 of the 27-level ancestor chain (ancestor e0a5b09f80625fb6), confirming an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in the winner's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the format selector is read but the case-4 arm is reached without any constant-4 comparison (e.g. format masked or computed) yet cmplog still resolves it far faster than naive — showing the divergence is not driven by I2S substitution of the literal 4.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact byte offset and width of the format selector inside the GDEF/ClassDef structure is inferred from harfbuzz table layout, not directly proven by the byte-diff section, so the precise offset of the I2S substitution is uncertain.
